steps.title: Identify the explicit chromium comparison
steps.content: The passage directly states that ferritic stainless steel contains a higher percentage of chromium (at least 10.5%) than does austenitic stainless steel. This is the key defining difference between the two steel types.

steps.title: Apply the comparison to the product applications
steps.content:
Since dishwashers use the 200 series (austenitic) and induction cookers use ferritic, choice D follows directly—dishwashers must have lower chromium than induction cookers. The other three all contradict the passage.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
